blocks.name: hero

contentModel.description: Full-bleed hero with background image, heading overlay, and optional CTA

structure.notes: Single image; for video variant, use a video link instead

cells: Heading (h1) + optional paragraph + optional CTA link
structure.notes: Rich text with heading, subtext, and button link

contentModel.authoringExample:
| Hero |
|---|
| ![hero background](/images/hero-lips.jpg) |
| # Jouw lippen, jouw keuze |
| Ontdek de JUVÉDERM® behandelingen |
| **[Vind je kliniek](/nl/find-a-clinic)** |

variantNotes.default: Static background image with text overlay
variantNotes.video: Use Hero (video) variant name; first cell contains video URL instead of image

blocks.css: hero.css — full-bleed background, gradient overlay for text readability, responsive image sizing, centered text
blocks.js: hero.js — decorates image as background, handles video variant (auto-play, loop, muted), lazy-loads video

blocks.name: accordion

contentModel.description: Expandable Q&A or collapsible content sections. Each row is one accordion item.

structure.notes: Col 1 = summary/trigger text, Col 2 = expandable detail. Rich text supports paragraphs, lists, sup references.

contentModel.authoringExample:
| Accordion |
|---|---|
| Wat is JUVÉDERM®? | JUVÉDERM® is een collectie van injecteerbare gezichtsvullers... |
| Hoe lang houdt het resultaat aan? | De resultaten kunnen tot 12-18 maanden aanhouden.^1^ |

blocks.css: accordion.css — expand/collapse animation, chevron indicator, focus styles, border between items
blocks.js: accordion.js — toggle open/close, optional single-open mode, ARIA attributes for accessibility

blocks.name: carousel

contentModel.description: Sliding content carousel. Each row is one slide with image and text content.

structure.notes: Each row = one slide. Col 1 = slide image, Col 2 = text content with heading, paragraph, and optional link.

contentModel.authoringExample:
| Carousel |
|---|---|
| ![lips treatment](/images/lips.jpg) | ## Lippen |
| | Creëer de perfecte pout met JUVÉDERM® |
| | [Meer informatie](/nl/treatment/lips) |

blocks.css: carousel.css — slide transitions, dot navigation, swipe support, responsive sizing
blocks.js: carousel.js — slide logic, touch/swipe, dot indicators, auto-advance optional

blocks.name: cards

contentModel.description: Grid of content cards. Each row is one card with optional image.

structure.notes: For no-images variant, omit image cell. Cards auto-layout into responsive grid.

contentModel.authoringExample:
| Cards |
|---|---|
| ![icon natural](/images/icon-natural.svg) | ## Natuurlijk resultaat |
| | JUVÉDERM® geeft een resultaat dat er natuurlijk uitziet en voelt. |

blocks.css: cards.css — responsive grid layout (2-3 columns), card borders/shadows, hover states
blocks.js: cards.js — minimal decoration, responsive column count

blocks.name: tabs

contentModel.description: Tabbed content sections. Each section with a tab-label in section metadata becomes a tab panel. Grouped under a Tabs block wrapper.

structure.notes: Each row = one tab. Col 1 = tab heading label, Col 2 = tab panel content.

contentModel.authoringExample:
| Tabs |
|---|---|
| VOLBELLA | JUVÉDERM® VOLBELLA met lidocaïne wordt gebruikt om fijne lijntjes te behandelen...^2^ |
| VOLIFT | JUVÉDERM® VOLIFT met lidocaïne herstelt het volume in het middengelaat...^3^ |

blocks.css: tabs.css — tab bar styling, active/inactive states, panel show/hide
blocks.js: tabs.js — tab switching, ARIA tablist/tab/tabpanel roles, keyboard navigation

blocks.name: columns

contentModel.description: Multi-column layout for side-by-side image + text. From EDS boilerplate.

structure.notes: 2 cells = 2 columns. Content in each cell renders as a column.

contentModel.authoringExample:
| Columns |
|---|---|
| ![treatment photo](/images/treatment.jpg) | ## Waarom JUVÉDERM®? |
| | Ons assortiment biedt... |

blocks.css: columns.css — flexbox/grid 2-col layout, responsive stacking on mobile
blocks.js: columns.js — minimal, adds column count class

blocks.name: topic-menu

contentModel.description: Sticky horizontal anchor navigation for FAQ sections. A single row with anchor links to page sections.

cells: List of anchor links (ul with a-hrefs to #section-ids)
structure.notes: Author creates an unordered list of links. Each link targets a section anchor on the same page.

contentModel.authoringExample:
| Topic Menu |
|---|
| - [Over Juvéderm®](#about-juvederm) |
| - [Kosten](#duration) |
| - [Langdurige resultaten](#results) |
| - [Veiligheid](#side-effects) |
| - [Zones van het gezicht](#facial-areas) |

blocks.css: topic-menu.css — sticky positioning, horizontal scroll on mobile, active state highlight, brand colors
blocks.js: topic-menu.js — intersection observer to highlight active section, smooth scroll on click

blocks.name: clinic-finder

contentModel.description: Interactive clinic search with Google Maps. Configuration block that renders a map + search + results UI.

structure.notes: Google Maps API key loaded from site config, not authored in document

cells: Vind een kliniek bij jou in de buurt
Voer je postcode of stad in
URL or API endpoint for clinic data
structure.notes: Endpoint that returns clinic JSON data

contentModel.authoringExample:
| Clinic Finder |
|---|---|
| heading | Vind een kliniek bij jou in de buurt |
| placeholder | Voer je postcode of stad in |
| data-source | /clinic-data.json |

blocks.css: clinic-finder.css — map container, search bar, results list, clinic cards, responsive layout
blocks.js: clinic-finder.js — Google Maps initialization, geocoding, clinic data fetch, distance sorting, result rendering
dependencies: Clinic data endpoint (to be determined — JSON spreadsheet or external API)

pageModel.notes: Full-bleed hero with auto-playing background video, h1 heading, and CTA

Collapsible product details / secondary info section
Primary CTA: 'Vind je kliniek' button — rendered as default content strong link
Regulatory footnotes with superscript references

metadataMapping.title: Vind een kliniek | Juvéderm® Netherlands
metadataMapping.description: Find the right clinic for you
metadataMapping.og:title: same as title

pageModel.notes: Entirely default content: heading, paragraphs, lists. No blocks needed.

metadataMapping.title: from source title
metadataMapping.description: from source meta description (may be empty)

sequences: default-content (h1 heading + contact info + address + links)
pageModel.notes: Simple contact info page — heading, address block, email/phone links. All default content.

model.description: EDS auto-loads header from /nav document. Structured as logo link + nested list for dropdown navigation + CTA button.
structure: CTA button: **[Vind je kliniek](/nl/find-a-clinic)** (strong link = primary button)
model.mobileNotes: Hamburger menu with slide-out navigation panel. Same link structure.

model.description: EDS auto-loads footer from /footer document. Contains CTA, social links, nav links, regulatory text, and legal links.
structure: Section 2 — Social media icons: Instagram, Facebook (SVG icons from /icons/)
Section 3 — Navigation links (same structure as header nav but flat list)
Section 4 — Regulatory/brand text: AbbVie/Allergan branding, disclaimer text
Section 5 — Legal links: Privacy, Terms, Disclaimer, Cookie settings (#onetrust), Clinic T&Cs
Section 6 — Copyright: © AbbVie [year]. All rights reserved.

globalMapping.title: Preserved from source page titles (trimmed of leading spaces)
globalMapping.og:title: Same as title
globalMapping.og:image: Re-hosted hero/share image per page (downloaded from source DAM)
globalMapping.twitter:title: Same as title
globalMapping.template: content-page (all pages use same AEM template)

bulkMetadata.description: Use bulk metadata spreadsheet (metadata.xlsx) for site-wide defaults: og:site_name, template, lang. Per-page metadata authored in document metadata block.

decisions.title: homepage / locale-root dedup
decisions.decision: Migrate once as homepage (/). Set up redirect from /nl → / in redirects.xlsx.
decisions.rationale: Both / and /nl serve identical content with the same canonical URL. Maintaining two identical pages creates authoring overhead and confusion. Single homepage with redirect is cleaner.

decisions.title: clinic-detail pages out of scope
decisions.decision: Mark clinic-detail pages as out-of-scope for static migration. The clinic-finder block will render individual clinic info dynamically.
decisions.rationale: 54+ dynamic pages generated by JS with CRM query params. Not suitable for static content migration. The clinic-finder custom block will handle clinic display at runtime from a data source.

decisions.title: clinic-finder Google Maps integration
decisions.decision: Build custom clinic-finder block with Google Maps JavaScript API. Clinic data served from a JSON spreadsheet or external API endpoint. API key stored in site configuration (not authored).
decisions.rationale: Source site uses Google Maps via emu-location-services. Custom block is required — no Block Collection equivalent. Spreadsheet-as-JSON provides a simple, authorable data source for clinic records.

decisions.title: cookie consent via delayed.js
decisions.decision: Integrate OneTrust cookie consent script in delayed.js. Not a content block — it's a global script integration.
decisions.rationale: Cookie consent must not impact page load performance. delayed.js is the standard EDS pattern for third-party scripts. OneTrust SDK loaded after page content is interactive.

decisions.title: video in hero, not standalone block
decisions.decision: Handle homepage video as a Hero (video) variant rather than a separate video block.
decisions.rationale: Only the homepage uses video, and only as a hero background. A standalone video block is unnecessary. Hero variant keeps the palette minimal and the authoring model simple.

decisions.title: /nl/clinics and /nl/clinic are clinic-finder aliases
decisions.decision: Set up redirects: /nl/clinics → /nl/find-a-clinic and /nl/clinic → /nl/find-a-clinic in redirects.xlsx.
decisions.rationale: All three URLs serve the same clinic finder UI. Single canonical page avoids duplication.

decisions.title: topic-menu as custom block (not section style)
decisions.decision: Implement topic-menu as a lightweight custom block rather than a section style on default content.
decisions.rationale: A dedicated block provides clearer authoring semantics, JS for intersection-observer highlighting, and potential reuse on other long-form pages.

decisions.title: prefer default content over blocks for simple pages
decisions.decision: Legal and contact pages use exclusively default content (headings, paragraphs, lists, links). No blocks authored.
decisions.rationale: These pages are simple text. Wrapping them in blocks would add authoring complexity for no benefit. EDS default content renders clean semantic HTML.

decisions.title: Google Fonts → self-hosted fonts
decisions.decision: Download Assistant and Roboto fonts from Google Fonts, host in /fonts/ directory, declare in fonts.css.
decisions.rationale: Self-hosting fonts avoids third-party DNS lookups and improves LCP. Standard EDS performance best practice.

decisions.title: analytics via delayed.js
decisions.decision: Load Google Tag Manager, Google Analytics, and Facebook Pixel scripts via delayed.js.
decisions.rationale: All analytics/marketing scripts are non-critical for page functionality. Loading via delayed.js protects Core Web Vitals scores.

pilot.rationale: Homepage is the highest-traffic page and exercises the most blocks (hero, carousel, cards, accordion). Validates core infrastructure.
Lips is the primary treatment page. Validates the treatment-page template (hero, columns, tabs, cards, accordion).
FAQ validates accordion (heaviest usage), topic-menu custom block, and anchor navigation.
Simple text page — quick win to validate default content rendering and metadata.

redirectsOnly.reason: Duplicate of homepage — identical content, same canonical

outOfScope.reason: Dynamic pages generated from CRM data. Handled at runtime by the clinic-finder block, not as static content pages.

sectionStyles.name: hero
sectionStyles.description: Full-bleed background image section, used for page heroes

sectionStyles.name: highlight
sectionStyles.description: Accent background color section for feature callouts

sectionStyles.name: qa-section
sectionStyles.description: FAQ topic group section with id anchor for topic-menu navigation

sectionStyles.name: centered
sectionStyles.description: Centered text content layout (legal, contact pages)

sectionStyles.name: footnotes
sectionStyles.description: Small-text regulatory footnotes section at bottom of pages

ctaPattern.description: Primary CTA is 'Vind je kliniek' (Find your clinic) linking to clinic finder. Secondary CTAs link to treatment pages. Standard EDS button patterns: strong-wrapped link = primary, em-wrapped = secondary.

imageStrategy.notes: Download all images from source AEM DAM. Commit to DA content or repo. EDS CDN handles responsive optimization automatically. SVG icons go to /icons/ directory.

brandConventions.registeredMark: Always include ® on first mention per page

fonts.strategy: Download and self-host in /fonts/, declare in fonts.css, preload primary font for LCP
